body.title: [triage] AiderDevice — wrap aider as a rack builder (Granny-dispatched, throwaway-clone, branch-not-main, CC-validated)
body.description:
**Problem:** aider is PROVEN a viable builder on our stack (T-aider-builder-first-run + T-aider-multifile-orientation-test: 4/4 GREEN, multi-file orientation held, both models, qwen 2.2x faster, $0 Hex). The remaining step to 'a builder we're happy with' running on the swarm is wrapping it as a rack device that takes a real ticket and produces a validated close — and proving it on a REAL UU ticket against a repo clone (the large-repo + contamination surface the sandboxes did not exercise).

**Proposed shape (subject to Akien's design answers below):** A new devices/aider/ BaseDevice+BaseShim wrapping the aider_smoke invocation logic generalized to (ticket_id, repo_clone) -> aider headless -> result. Granny dispatches role=builder to it (handshake, not spawn — per project_granny_pull_dispatch_and_alarm_model); throwaway clone per ticket (~/dicksimnel pattern, RELATIVE paths — 124553ee contamination lesson); commits to a target BRANCH not main (T-ds-builder-target-branch-not-main); close is CC-VALIDATED (unvalidated builder throughput just shifts the queue to CC — project note). Model = qwen3-coder:30b default (fastest+capable), devstral fallback.

**HIGH-INERTIA — needs Akien pre-approval before sprint (new device + Granny dispatch integration changes how work is routed). Best started FRESH (L architectural build; context-depth confabulation risk). DESIGN QUESTIONS FOR AKIEN:**
- Q1: Does AiderDevice call aider via subprocess (keep aider fully external, no imports — matches the proven smoke path) or via aider's Python API? (Recommend subprocess — preserves the no-aider-imports rule + the proven invocation.)
- Q2: One AiderDevice instance per laptop (the swarm shells), or one logical device that fans out? (Ties to the 3-laptop spin-up.)
- Q3: Corpus capture — build the Stage-2 OpenAI-compatible proxy endpoint FIRST (so aider bytes land in io_corpus) or ship the device black-box and add capture after? (Recommend black-box first, capture as a fast follow — do not block the working builder on the endpoint.)
- Q4: Validation gate — CC validates every aider close inline, or a cheaper automated gate (tests-green + diff-scope check) with CC spot-checks? (Validation capacity is the real constraint on swarm throughput.)

**Affected files:** new devices/aider/ (device.py, shim.py, runner); Granny dispatch (role=builder routing) — HIGH inertia; config profile. Generalizes devlab/claudecode/aider_smoke.py.
**Design rules:** device.py/shim.py design center (BaseDevice/BaseShim); no aider imports into unseen_university/; Granny = handshake not spawn; branch-not-main; no SQLite; log every dispatch/close (interface crossings).
**Scope boundary:** the device + Granny wiring + ONE real-ticket proof run against a repo clone. Proxy-endpoint corpus capture + 3-laptop fleet spin-up are separate follow-ups.
**Completion criteria:**
- AiderDevice accepts a dispatched ticket, runs aider (qwen@Hex) on a throwaway clone, commits to a branch, returns a result for CC validation.
- Proven on >=1 REAL UU ticket end-to-end (real repo clone, not a sandbox) — edits applied, tests green, branch pushed, CC-validated close.
- Granny routes role=builder to it via handshake (no spawn).
**Test plan:** red->green on the device's own logic (dispatch->run->result contract); the real-ticket run is the behavioral proof (a real failing/incomplete ticket -> aider -> green + validated).

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.intention: I intend that aider — proven viable — becomes a Granny-dispatched rack builder that produces validated closes on real tickets against real repo clones, giving us a working swarm builder NOW while DS is leveled to match. Serves the aider-builder-first standing intention and the swarm spin-up.
